steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is the explicit frequency contrast: “common among prokaryotes” versus “it has rarely been observed among eukaryotes”—directly comparing how often the phenomenon appears in each organism type.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice A captures the exact function: the sentence “contrasts the frequency with which a biological phenomenon has been detected in two categories of organisms.” This describes the frequency opposition (common versus rarely observed) between prokaryotes and eukaryotes. The others fail: B wrongly claims two phenomena are shown as similar, C overstates that the sentence explains why the perception is flawed (it only presents the observation), and D misidentifies the focus as process mechanics rather than frequency. That matches choice A — A is correct.
